data.title: Corteva sees higher restructuring charges, plans to cease production at Spanish site
data.description:
Corteva (CTVA) disclosed Friday it ‌expects higher costs from its multi-year crop protection operations restructuring program ​and plans to cease ​production activities at its site in Asturias, Spain.

The company ​said it now expects to record total pre-tax restructuring and asset-related charges of $750M-$815M, including at least $300M of costs related to exiting the production activities and ceasing operations, compared to prior estimates of $650M-$700M.

Corteva (CTVA) said ​it expects ​cash outflows of $400M-$443M related to the restructuring, primarily ​for decommissioning and demolition, contract terminations, and the payment of severance and related benefits.

Separately, Corteva (CTVA) said Thursday it agreed to pay $85M to resolve farmers' claims in a class action lawsuit accusing it of ​rigging the market for some crop protection products.

The lawsuit alleged Corteva (CTVA) and Syngenta used loyalty programs with distributors to impact competition from generic manufacturers, keeping prices ​of their crop protection products artificially high.

The company denied any wrongdoing ​in agreeing to settle the lawsuit, which was filed ⁠in 2022; the deal requires approval from a judge.

data.title: Inside Lockheed Martin's $2.293B win: F-35 jet long-term sustainment contract
data.description:
• Lockheed Martin won a $2.293B contract for F-35 sustainment support services on Friday.
  • The contract covers F-35 sustainment work, including site activation, fleet management, interim contractor support, and reliability improvements for U.S. Air Force, Navy, Marine, and allied FMS and F-35 program partners.
  • The work is expected to be completed by December 2028, with Naval Air Systems Command managing the contract.
  • No funds are obligated at award; payments will be made as individual orders are issued.
  • On the market side, the stock closed ~1.52% lower at ~$540.33.

data.title: Gold gains on growing U.S.-Iran deal hopes, tempering rate hike expectations
data.description:
Gold futures surged 3% Friday on hopes for a peace deal in the Middle East, as a preliminary deal to end the conflict between the U.S. and Iran that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz appears within reach.

The news bolstered investor risk appetite, helping lift the stock market while pushing crude oil, Treasury yields, and the dollar lower, easing expectations—for now—of rate hikes from the U.S. Federal Reserve.

The U.S., Iran, and chief mediator Pakistan all agreed a deal to end the war was nearly complete, with an initial agreement that would open up a 60-day period during which Iran would open the Strait of Hormuz and the U.S. would wind back its blockade of Iran's ports and commerce.

Negotiations would be held on a final nuclear deal and the sanctions relief Iran would receive under it, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing an unnamed senior U.S. official.

"With oil prices coming down sharply, alongside hopes that the Strait of Hormuz will reopen, that has seen investors price out the chance of rapid rate hikes this year," Deutsche Bank said in a note.

Despite Friday's rebound, gold still finished lower for the week, as a flare-up of attacks between the U.S. and Iran in recent days and U.S. consumer price data led to inflation fears that could ​prompt central banks to keep interest rates higher, raising the opportunity cost of holding non-yielding precious metals.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for June delivery jumped 3% on Friday to $4,215.00/oz, while front-month Comex June silver (XAGUSD:CUR) surged 6.2% to $67.859/oz, snapping five-session losing streaks for both metals.

For the full week, gold and silver fell 2.8% and 1.6%, respectively.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (DUST), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SGDM), (SGDJ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: BioNxt targets up to C$2M in private placement
data.description:
• BioNxt Solutions (BNXTF) said on Friday it plans a non-brokered private placement to raise up to C$2M under Canada's Listed Issuer Financing Exemption.
  • The company will offer up to 6.06M units at C$0.33 each, with every unit comprising one common share and one warrant exercisable at C$0.50 for 24 months.
  • BioNxt said proceeds will be used for its European research and development programs, operations, working capital, and general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close before July 27, subject to regulatory approvals.

data.title: The Trade Desk, Publicis settle dispute over DSP fees
data.description:
The Trade Desk (TTD) and Publicis (PUBGY) have settled their dispute over “previous differences,” enabling Publicis to recommend the ad platform to its clients.

The rift began in March when an audit commissioned by Publicis (PUBGY) found that The Trade Desk (TTD) was improperly stacking its Demand-Side Platform ((DSP)) fees—the cost advertisers pay to use programmatic ad software to buy digital media—in violation of an agreement with Publicis (PUBGY). 

Publicis (PUBGY) subsequently advised its clients to stop spending on The Trade Desk, driving TTD’s stock down double digits.

But after months of bickering between the two, The Trade Desk (TTD) and Publicis (PUBGY) have settled their differences and resumed business as usual. The joint statement did not mention how the dispute was resolved or if any terms were agreed to.

“Publicis and The Trade Desk were able to address previous differences as identified in the [Publicis] audit and can now recommend TTD to our clients along with other DSP partners,” read a statement that was first shared with Ad Age. “Publicis and The Trade Desk are now focused on moving forward and continue to maintain a mutual commitment to delivering measurable outcomes for advertisers.”

The development pushed TTD shares 2% higher on Friday and Publicis Groupe (PUBGY) by 1.6%.

data.title: Binance 'bStocks' twist: Trade these U.S. stocks like crypto; 24/7 access coming
data.description:
Binance Exchange (BNB-USD) announced the launch of “bStocks,” a new set of fully backed tokenized securities issued by BTech Holdings Limited, a Binance group affiliate. 

Reportedly, the exchange is set to turn selected U.S. stocks into crypto-style tokens on the blockchain so they can be traded 24/7, stored in personal wallets, and used in DeFi apps.

At launch, the initial bStocks lineup includes Circle Internet Group (CRCL) → (CRCLB/USDT), Micron Technology (MU) → (MUB/USDT), NVIDIA (NVDA) → (NVDAB/USDT), Sandisk (SNDK) → (SNDKB/USDT), Tesla (TSLA) → (TSLAB/USDT), and SpaceX (SPCX) → (SPCXB/USDT) is also planned.

Each token is backed 1:1 by underlying shares held with a regulated custodian, and users can convert between stocks and tokens via Binance broker-dealer Nest Trading Limited, reported PR Newswire.

The exchange also said that users can start investing with fractional exposure from just $5, making access broader and more flexible.

Co-CEO Richard Teng stated, “bStocks are the next step in that evolution, bringing real-world assets on-chain while giving users greater portability and self-custody.”

The platform is also offering zero maker fees for selected pairs for a limited time, including MUB/USDT, CRCLB/USDT, NVDAB/USDT, SNDKB/USDT, and TSLAB/USDT from the (June 11–Aug 31, 2026) period.

Moreover, corporate actions like splits and dividends are automatically adjusted for seamless exposure.

Investors should note: bStocks is already launched and operational, but its full stock lineup is rolling out progressively, with some assets already active and others planned or conditional.

data.title: Laser Photonics regains Nasdaq compliance as 10-Q delay issue resolved
data.description:
• Laser Photonics (LASE) received a Nasdaq notice on Friday, confirming that the company has regained full compliance with listing rules requiring timely submission of periodic financial reports.
  • The company had earlier fallen out of compliance due to the late filing of its Q1 2026 Form 10-Q for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, which was later submitted on June 11, 2026.
  • Following the filing, Nasdaq reviewed and confirmed compliance on June 12, 2026, closing the issue with no further action required.
  • The stock traded ~5.71% higher at ~$2.22 in the after-hours trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Meta to curb employees AI usage — The Information
data.description:
• To rein in ballooning data costs, Meta Platforms (META) will begin limiting employees use of AI tokens.
  • The company is reportedly building an internal platform that will track AI usage, set budgets, and establish limits on token spending, according to The Information.
  • The guardrails also include automated alerts for unusual spending spikes.
  • “This is a priority, and we’re focused on using AI to help employees with their day-to-day work,” according to a statement from the company shared with The Information.
  • Meta (META) joins Microsoft (MSFT) and Uber Technologies (UBER) who have also established AI spending curbs.

data.title: Norsk Hydro declares new force majeure on Qatar aluminum - Reuters
data.description:
Norsk Hydro (NHYDY) (NHYKF) has declared a second force majeure on aluminum sales from Qatar after its Qatalum joint venture unexpectedly ended a marketing ​agreement with the Norwegian company, Reuters ‌reported Friday.

Norsk Hydro (NHYDY) (NHYKF), which owns 50% in the 648K metric tons/year Qatalum project alongside Qatar Aluminum Manufacturing, issued a first force majeure three months ago after ​the outbreak of the Iran war interrupted the plant's gas ​supply and Qatalum initiated a shutdown.

That force majeure remains in place even after Qatalum subsequently received enough gas ​to operate at 60% of capacity, but a second force majeure reportedly was issued to Hydro's (NHYDY) (NHYKF) customers on Friday related to its commercial arrangements with Qatalum.

"Despite Hydro's efforts to make Qatalum withdraw its ‌notice ⁠of termination, Qatalum has refused to do so and Qatalum has informed Hydro that it will not deliver metal under the relevant agreements," the force majeure notice reportedly said.

It was unclear why Qatalum had terminated the agreement, the report said.

data.title: DexCom's OTC CGM Stelo gains pediatric approval
data.description:
• The US FDA has approved DexCom's (DXCM) over-the-counter continuous glucose monitor Stelo for use in children two years and older.
  • The indication is for children who receive oral medication for diabetes.
  • Stelo consists of a wearable sensor that is paired to a smartphone app that tracks and provides glucose data every 15 minutes. The sensor is replaced every 15 days.
  • Stelo is the first OTC CGM approved for children.
